diagrams.title: Basic Tree Structure with All Terminology
diagrams.description: A labeled tree diagram showing all key concepts: root, internal nodes, leaf nodes, parent-child relationships, siblings, levels, edges, height
elements: Level 1: Contains parent nodes with their own children
Edges: Lines connecting parent to children
Siblings: Nodes sharing same parent at Level 1
Height: Longest path from root to any leaf
diagrams.purpose: Visualize all tree terminology in one comprehensive diagram - helps memorize relationships between concepts

diagrams.title: Tree vs Linear Structure Comparison
diagrams.description: Side-by-side comparison showing: (a) Linear array with sequential indices, (b) Tree with hierarchical branching
elements: Tree: Root branches to multiple children at each level
Visualizes why trees are better for hierarchies
diagrams.purpose: Understand why trees are non-linear and how they differ fundamentally from arrays and linked lists

diagrams.title: Binary Tree Example
diagrams.description: A complete binary tree with each node having at most 2 children - left and right child clearly marked
elements: Root with left and right child
Each node explicitly shows left and right pointers
diagrams.purpose: Reinforce binary tree definition - each node can have 0, 1, or 2 children, commonly labeled as left and right
